name: hf-voiceover
description: Generate the voiceover audio and word-level timing for a motion-graphics project. Reads the beat-structured 02-script.md, synthesizes the full narration in ONE ai-gen TTS call (Kokoro) to a single narration.wav, then transcribes it ONCE with ai-gen ASR (Wizper) and splits the word-timings back into beats — producing word-level timings in 04-timing.json for caption sync and beat pacing. Use during the VOICEOVER phase (phase 4) of a narrated video, or to RE-VOICE an existing project with a different voice. Keyless via the SL8 ai-gen proxy; if TTS is unreachable it renders silent and records the fallback — it never prompts the user.

inputs.name: script
inputs.description: artifacts/<project-name>/02-script.md — the beat-structured script written by hf-script (the VO lines per beat are the narration source).

inputs.name: voice
inputs.description: A Kokoro voice id (e.g. am_michael, af_nova). Default = the voice in context.md, else am_michael (a neutral American narrator). See references/ai-gen-audio.md for the voice list.

inputs.name: model
inputs.description: The ai-gen TTS model slug. Default fal-ai/kokoro/american-english (confirmed keyless in-sandbox).

outputs.name: voiceover
outputs.description: One continuous narration wav for the whole project, generated by a single ai-gen TTS call, that hf-build references and hf-render muxes. Per-beat slices are not produced by default (one VO track is the HyperFrames-native model); a downstream skill that needs them can cut them locally from narration.wav using the beat boundaries in 04-timing.json (ffmpeg, no extra network calls).

outputs.name: timing
outputs.description: Word-level timings ([{text,start,end}] per beat plus a flat absolute-time words[] track) derived from a single ai-gen ASR pass and split into beats by order, used for caption sync and beat pacing.
